title: Bryan G. West
content:
People.ContactEmail bwest@mccarthy.ca
Bryan G. West is a nationally recognized authority on construction, infrastructure, and project dispute law. A unique practitioner, who argues the case, writes the book, and helps shape the law.
Bryan G. West is a nationally recognized litigator and authority on construction, infrastructure, and project dispute law. He is also a leading author in the field, writing and co-authoring major Canadian texts on Canadian and Alberta construction and infrastructure laws, including as author of Heintzman, West and Goldsmith on Canadian Building Contracts , Builders’ Liens in Alberta , and Alberta Construction and Infrastructure Law.
Bryan is a partner in McCarthy Tétrault LLP’s Litigation Group based in Calgary, where he maintains a national practice focused on high-value commercial disputes with subject matter specialization in construction, infrastructure, and project disputes. He acts for regional, national, and multinational clients, including corporations, governments, developers, institutional investors, owners, contractors, subcontractors, engineers, architects, and suppliers, in high-stakes, multi-party mandates across Alberta, British Columbia, and Ontario.
Bryan is known for combining industry knowledge, commercial judgment, and strategic advocacy to help clients resolve technically complex disputes involving competing expert evidence, whether through trial, arbitration, mediation, negotiation, or early dispute management.

#### Construction, Infrastructure & Project Disputes
Bryan leads some of Canada’s most significant construction and infrastructure mandates. His project experience spans urban developments, including commercial, residential, and mixed-use towers, hotels, and resorts; energy projects, including oil and gas, mining, renewable energy, and industrial facilities; and large-scale infrastructure undertakings, including highways, reservoirs, stadiums, metros, and light rail transit systems. He regularly advises on disputes involving public-private partnerships, novel procurement structures, and major public infrastructure delivery.
Bryan brings a practical approach to construction disputes at every stage of the project lifecycle. He advises on risk allocation and contract formation, manages disputes as they emerge during project delivery, and advances claims through negotiation, mediation, adjudication, arbitration, trial, appeal, judgment, and award enforcement. His transactional experience includes drafting and negotiating complex construction contracts, including CCDC supplementary conditions and government procurement and infrastructure template agreements in Alberta, British Columbia, and Ontario.
Bryan’s construction disputes practice encompasses delay and cost-overrun claims, scope changes, indemnity issues, design errors, product and facility defects and failures, warranty claims, insurance-related issues, and construction and builders’ liens, including holdback, set-off, prompt payment, adjudication, lien fund recovery, lien bond litigation, and claims arising under Alberta’s Prompt Payment and Construction Liens Act and Public Works Act .
Recent representative mandates include:
- VINCI Construction: acting as lead construction and dispute counsel for VINCI GeoInfrastructure Canada in the resolution of cost and schedule claims respecting one of Alberta’s most significant public improvement projects;
- City of Calgary : acting as lead construction counsel in connection with the construction and procurement of a high-profile civic arena and entertainment district redevelopment, with a project value in excess of CAD $1 billion;
- QuadReal: acting for a major real estate developer in active dispute management on one of Canada’s most ambitious mixed-use urban redevelopment projects, with a project value in excess of CAD $5 billion; and
- Glencore: acting for a resource company in construction claims arising from a major industrial facility redevelopment in British Columbia valued in excess of CAD $200 million.

#### Commercial Litigation & Arbitration
Bryan represents national and multinational corporations in high-value disputes involving complex contracts, fiduciary duties, fraud, negligence, and joint venture breakdowns.
Bryan’s practice is defined by his ability to advance high-stakes mandates across several key Canadian sectors:
- Energy: Bryan acts for clients in disputes involving operating agreements, royalty and lease disputes, mineral rights, oil sands operations, midstream infrastructure, and chemical processing facilities.
- Commercial Real Estate & Leasing: Bryan represents owners, landlords, developers, and institutional tenants in all aspects of lease enforcement, anticipatory breach, and termination litigation. His appellate advocacy has actively shaped Alberta case law in this area.
- Municipal, Planning & Development: Bryan advises municipalities and developers on zoning, subdivision appeals, and bylaw enforcement. He appears regularly before municipal appeal boards and councils on behalf of residential, mixed-use, and industrial developers.
- Specialized Fraud & Misappropriation: Bryan leads sensitive, cross-jurisdictional investigations and litigation, including multi-jurisdictional fraud actions involving allegations of fraudulent invoicing and misappropriation of corporate funds.
Recent representative mandates include:
- lead counsel for a major energy company in the fully successful defence of a complex commercial arbitration involving claims in excess of CAD $40 million arising from joint venture cost-allocation disputes at a major Alberta gas-processing facility; and
- lead counsel in a cross-jurisdictional fraud action involving allegations of fraudulent invoicing and misappropriation of corporate funds, with claims and counterclaims exceeding CAD $20 million, pursued concurrently in Alberta and British Columbia.
Bryan’s publications distinguish him as both a practitioner and a legal author in Canadian construction law. His books and annual publications address core issues in building contracts, builders’ liens, prompt payment, adjudication, and construction liens, and are relied on by courts, counsel, and industry participants across Canada. Bryan is the author of:
- Heintzman, West and Goldsmith on Canadian Building Contracts (updates published quarterly 1966 – current)
- Builders’ Liens in Alberta (2015 – 2021)
- Prompt Payment, Adjudication and Construction Liens in Alberta (2022 – 2025)
- Alberta Construction and Infrastructure Law (coming late 2026)
- Chambers Canada, Leading Lawyer: Litigation: General Commercial (Alberta)
- Canadian Legal Lexpert Directory - Leading Lawyer: Construction Law
- Benchmark Litigation Canada, Future Star
- Acritas Stars, Stand-out Lawyer
- Calgary Herald, Top 20 Most Compelling Calgarians
- Government of Alberta, Alberta Centennial Medal
- University of Calgary, Order of the University of Calgary

#### Background & Community
A native of Banff, Alberta, Bryan holds a BA (Political Science) from the University of Calgary, where he received the Order of the University of Calgary, and a JD from Queen’s University. He was called to the Alberta Bar in 2011.
Bryan is a committed community volunteer. His affiliations include Pro Bono Students Canada (Supervising Lawyer), the University of Calgary Board of Governors (Past Governor), the University of Calgary Senate (Past Senator), the Forum for Albertans (Trustee and Director), and the Ministry of Advanced Education’s Learning Alberta Steering Committee.
